--- title: "SAT" editor: visual --- # Data Prep Alright, lets see what this dataset looks like. I will first import the entire dataset, then clean it up, and then merge it with the master / Northwest graduate dataset.
::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} sat.original <- read_csv('/Users/kellyasche/Library/CloudStorage/GoogleDrive-kasche@ruralmn.org/My Drive/Research/FY25-27/FY26/OHE Journey to meaningful employment/Data/SLEDS/SAT/AdvancedPlacementSATPSAT_SAT.csv') %>% mutate(PersonID = as.integer(PersonID)) kable(head(sat.original)) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} | PersonID| TestTimeID|TestType |ExamSubject | ExamGrade| |--------:|----------:|:--------|:---------------------------------------------|---------:| | 4900649| 20150630|SAT |SAT_CR_HC | 550| | 10691183| 20170630|SAT |SAT_Cross_Test_Score_History_SocialStudies_MR | 33| | 1619958| 20191005|SAT |SAT_Subscore_Context_HC | 10| | 6026739| 20210508|SAT |rSAT_ERW_Score_MR | 640| | 9243097| 20180630|SAT |SAT_Subscore_ProblemSolving_MR | 13| | 10782444| 20170630|SAT |SAT_Cross_Test_Score_History_SocialStudies_MR | 27| ::: ```{.r .cell-code} kable(names(sat.original)) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} |x | |:-----------| |PersonID | |TestTimeID | |TestType | |ExamSubject | |ExamGrade | ::: :::
There are 152,927 rows and 5 columns. The definitions of the columns are; - SATTimeID: Time of the test - TestType: SAT - ExamSubject: subject - ExamGrade: Exam grades are all over the place here and there's no definition in the data dictionary. Okay, since I don't have any information related to how the grading system works, I will just utilize this dataset to code wither a PersonID has taken an SAT test.
::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} sat.taken <- sat.original %>% mutate(sat.taken = 1) %>% select(PersonID, sat.taken) %>% distinct(PersonID, .keep_all = TRUE) kable(head(sat.taken)) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} | PersonID| sat.taken| |--------:|---------:| | 4900649| 1| | 10691183| 1| | 1619958| 1| | 6026739| 1| | 9243097| 1| | 10782444| 1| ::: ```{.r .cell-code} kable(names(sat.taken)) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} |x | |:---------| |PersonID | |sat.taken | ::: :::
Now we will join it with the master dataset.
::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} master.8 <- read_csv("Data/SLEDS/Masters/Master-7.csv") %>% mutate(PersonID) %>% left_join(sat.taken, by = "PersonID") %>% mutate(sat.taken = ifelse(is.na(sat.taken), 0, sat.taken), Dem_Desc = fct_relevel(Dem_Desc, "Town/rural mix", "Urban/town/rural mix", "Entirely urban")) sat.master <- read_csv("Data/SLEDS/Masters/Master-1.csv") %>% select(PersonID) %>% left_join(sat.taken, by = "PersonID") %>% mutate(sat.taken = ifelse(is.na(sat.taken), 0, sat.taken)) write_csv(sat.master, "Data/SLEDS/Masters/sat_master.csv") kable(head(master.8)) ``` ::: | |Work Experience/Career Exploration (age 14-15 on I | |Work-Experience-Disadvantaged | |Youth Apprenticeship | |cte.achievement | |english.learner | |MCA.M | |MCA.R | |MCA.S | |sat.taken | ::: :::
As expected there are 65,650 rows and 52 columns. # Summary of SAT data Lets check to see what percentage of the master dataset has taken the SAT. There is a tiny percentage of the dataset that has taken the SAT, 0.8%. With this information I don't see a need to check whether there is a significant difference in students taking the SAT based on RUCA category or EDR. Even if there is a significant difference, there just isn't enough observations to make a difference.
::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} sat.total <- master.8 %>% group_by(sat.taken) %>% summarize(n = n()) %>% ungroup() %>% mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) datatable(sat.total, class = "cell-border stripe", filter = "top", rownames = FALSE, options = list(columnDefs = list(list(className = "dt-center", targets = 0:1)))) %>% formatPercentage(3, digits = 1) %>% formatCurrency(2, digits = 0, "") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} write_csv(master.8, "Data/SLEDS/Masters/Master-8.csv") ``` :::
